info.title: Robinhood Unified API Map
info.description: Combined Robinhood map: official Robinhood Crypto Trading OpenAPI plus browser-backed brokerage/account route map. Crypto operations should use official Ed25519 signing; brokerage/account routes use caller-owned web/brokerage auth.

post.description:
Places a new crypto trading order with an order type. 

 **Note**: Depending on the type used in the request body, you must include the respective order configuration in the request body.

For order configurations that support both `asset_quantity` or `quote_amount`, only one can be present in the request body.
Cancels an open crypto trading order.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Order ID, unique per order

text/plain.example: Cancel request was submitted for order {id}

get.description:
Fetch a single bid and ask price per symbol, representing the best available price across our partner market makers. This price does not take into account the order size, and may not be the final execution price.

The bid and ask prices include a spread. The buy spread is the percent difference between the ask and the mid price. The sell spread is the percent difference between the bid and the mid price.

Note that in the v2 endpoint, **Place crypto orders with fee tiers**, partner exchanges provide prices instead of market makers, with orders routed accordingly. For more information on routing, visit our [<u>Help Center</u>](https://robinhood.com/us/en/support/articles/crypto-order-routing/).

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description:
List of trading pair symbol(s) to retrieve data for.

Multiple symbols can be provided by using the "symbol" parameter with different values. Ensure that the symbol(s) are provided in all uppercase.

For example, **?symbol=BTC-USD&symbol=ETH-USD**

get.description:
This endpoint returns the estimated total cost or credit for a particular symbol, book side, and asset quantity. You can include a list of quantities in a single request to retrieve the price for various hypothetical order sizes. 

The estimated price represents the expected execution price if you were to subsequently place an order. To estimate the cost for a Buy order, request an Ask quote. If you are preparing to place a Sell order, request a Bid quote. The execution price may vary due to market volatility and once executed the transaction may not be undone.

The bid and ask prices are the best prices our market makers provide for an order, inclusive of a spread. The buy spread is the percent difference between the ask and the mid price. The sell spread is the percent difference between the bid and the mid price.

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description:
A string matching a valid currency pair, for example BTC-USD. Ensure that the symbol is provided in all uppercase.

Only USD symbols are accepted.

parameters.name: side
parameters.description: Order side. e.g. bid, ask, or both.

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description:
Quantity must be between `min_order_size` and `max_order_size` as defined in our `Get Crypto Trading Pairs` endpoint.

 A max of 10 quantities can be specified for each request.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of crypto trading accounts for the authenticated user. Returns account details including buying power, status, and fee tier information.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Optional cursor for pagination. Use the value from `next` or `previous` in the response to navigate through pages.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Optional page size limit. Specifies the number of accounts to return per page. Maximum value is determined by system configuration.

get.description: Fetch a paginated list of available trading pairs for crypto trading. Returns trading pair details including price increments, order size limits, and tradability status. Trading pairs can be filtered by symbol.

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description:
Optional. Filter by trading pair symbol(s). Example: `symbol=BTC-USD`. Multiple symbols can be provided by using the "symbol" parameter with different values. Ensure that the symbol(s) are provided in all uppercase.

For example, `?symbol=BTC-USD&symbol=ETH-USD`.

If the "symbol" parameter is not provided, this endpoint will return all tradable currency pairs.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Optional cursor for pagination. Use the value from `next` or `previous` in the response to navigate through pages.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Optional page size limit. Specifies the number of results to return per page. Maximum value is determined by system configuration.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of crypto holdings for a specific account. Returns the total quantity and available quantity for each asset held in the account. The `account_number` query parameter is required.

parameters.name: account_number
parameters.description: **Required.** The account number to retrieve holdings for. This parameter must be provided.

parameters.name: asset_code
parameters.description: Optional. Filter holdings by specific asset code (e.g., "BTC").

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Optional cursor for pagination. Use the value from `next` or `previous` in the response to navigate through pages.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Optional page size limit. Specifies the number of results to return per page. Maximum value is determined by system configuration.

get.description: Fetch a single bid and ask price per symbol, representing the best available price across our partner exchanges. This price does not take into account the order size or fee, and may not be the final execution price.

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description:
List of trading pair symbol(s) to retrieve data for.

Multiple symbols can be provided by using the "symbol" parameter with different values. Ensure that the symbol(s) are provided in all uppercase. Only USD symbols that have `is_api_tradable=true` as defined in our Get Crypto Trading Pairs endpoint are accepted.

For example, `?symbol=BTC-USD&symbol=ETH-USD`

get.description:
This endpoint returns the estimated total cost or credit for a particular symbol, book side, asset quantity, and fee. You can include a list of quantities in a single request to retrieve the price for various hypothetical order sizes.

The estimated price represents the expected execution price if you were to subsequently place an order. The execution price may vary due to market volatility and once executed the transaction may not be undone.

The bid and ask prices are the best prices our partner exchanges provide for an order, excluding the fee.

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: A string matching a valid currency pair, for example `BTC-USD`. Ensure that the symbol is provided in all uppercase. Only USD symbols that have `is_api_tradable=true` as defined in our Get Crypto Trading Pairs endpoint are accepted.

parameters.name: side
parameters.description: Order side: `bid`, `ask`, or `both`.

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description: Quantity must be smaller than `max_order_size` as defined in our Get Crypto Trading Pairs endpoint. Multiple quantities can be comma-separated. A max of 10 quantities can be specified for each request.

get.description: Fetch a list of orders for the current user in a specific account.

parameters.name: account_number
parameters.description: Required. The number of the crypto account to retrieve orders from.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Optional cursor for pagination.

parameters.name: created_at_start
parameters.description: Filter orders created on or after this timestamp (ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: created_at_end
parameters.description: Filter orders created on or before this timestamp (ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: updated_at_start
parameters.description: Filter orders updated on or after this timestamp (ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: updated_at_end
parameters.description: Filter orders updated on or before this timestamp (ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: Filter orders by trading pair symbol.

parameters.name: side
parameters.description: Filter orders by side.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter orders by type.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Filter orders by state.

post.description:
Place a new crypto trading order with an order type. This endpoint only supports placing orders on USD symbols that have `is_api_tradable=true` as defined in our Get Crypto Trading Pairs endpoint.

**Note:** Depending on the type used in the request body, you must include the respective order configuration in the request body.

For order configurations that support both `asset_quantity` or `quote_amount`, only one can be present in the request body.

parameters.name: account_number
parameters.description: Required. The number of the crypto account to place orders from.

post.description: Cancels an open crypto trading order.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the order to cancel.
